description: Need to sync /home/user/reports/ to /home/user/backup/reports/ but only the csv files — there's a bunch of temp xlsx junk in there I don't want copied over. rsync should do it, just haven't used the include/exclude flags before. Make sure it gets the subdirectories too.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/reports/ exists with the following structure:
  ```
  /home/user/reports/
  ├── q1/
  │   ├── sales.csv
  │   ├── sales_draft.xlsx
  │   └── inventory.csv
  ├── q2/
  │   ├── sales.csv
  │   └── notes.xlsx
  ├── summary.csv
  └── scratch.xlsx
  ```
- /home/user/backup/ exists but /home/user/backup/reports/ does not exist yet.
- All files and directories under /home/user/ are writable by the agent.
- rsync is installed.

Expected final state:
- /home/user/backup/reports/ exists with this structure:
  ```
  /home/user/backup/reports/
  ├── q1/
  │   ├── sales.csv
  │   └── inventory.csv
  ├── q2/
  │   └── sales.csv
  └── summary.csv
  ```
- All 4 csv files are present: summary.csv, q1/sales.csv, q1/inventory.csv, q2/sales.csv.
- Each csv file in backup is byte-identical to its source in /home/user/reports/.
- No xlsx files exist anywhere under /home/user/backup/reports/.

Invariants:
- /home/user/reports/ and all its contents unchanged.
- No files outside /home/user/backup/reports/ created or modified.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- `find /home/user/backup/reports -name "*.xlsx" | wc -l` must return 0.
- `find /home/user/backup/reports -name "*.csv" | wc -l` must return 4.
